questions.question: Explain, in simple terms, why a consensus mechanism is a fundamental requirement for a decentralized blockchain.
keyPoints: Prevents double-spending by ensuring only one valid transaction history exists.
Eliminates the need for a central, trusted authority to validate transactions.
Enables all distributed nodes to agree on the state of the ledger and maintain its integrity.

questions.question: Compare and contrast the Proof of Work (PoW) and Proof of Stake (PoS) consensus mechanisms, highlighting their key operational differences and a major advantage/disadvantage for each.
keyPoints: PoW relies on miners solving computational puzzles (hashing) to validate blocks and secure the network, which is energy-intensive but highly secure against attacks.
PoS relies on validators staking their own cryptocurrency to validate blocks, which is far more energy-efficient but can lead to wealth concentration.
A key difference is the resource used: PoW uses external energy/computation, while PoS uses internal capital (staked crypto) as a security deposit.

questions.question: Evaluate the 'Blockchain Trilemma' in the context of consensus mechanisms. Using specific examples, discuss how modern consensus protocols (e.g., Delegated Proof of Stake, Byzantine Fault Tolerance variants) attempt to achieve a balance between decentralization, security, and scalability.
keyPoints: The trilemma posits that it is difficult for a blockchain to achieve full decentralization, high security, and scalability simultaneously; optimizing for two often compromises the third.
Delegated Proof of Stake (e.g., EOS) improves scalability by reducing the number of validating nodes but is criticized for compromising decentralization (leading to cartels).

Practical Byzantine Fault Tolerance (pBFT) used in Hyperledger Fabric offers high throughput (scalability) and security for permissioned networks but sacrifices the open decentralization of public blockchains.
Hybrid models (e.g., Casper for Ethereum) and sharding are examples of ongoing innovations attempting to address all three aspects more effectively.
